rationale.R1.1: Positioning against prior work centrally involves reframing the contribution claim and design-space gap above the sentence level.
rationale.R1.4: The delegation is load-bearing on estimating how AgentFlow differs from the nearest prior systems and whether the claimed delta is defensible.
rationale.R1.7: Prior-work positioning requires attention to attribution, reference coverage, and whether the named research lines are represented adequately.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection must preserve uncertainty about unprovided prior-work details and the strength of the novelty claim.
rationale.RX.2: The artifact contains strong claims such as a previously unaddressed design point, so avoiding overclaiming is active.
rationale.RX.3: The task is limited to positioning against prior work, not rewriting or evaluating experiments.
rationale.RX.4: Mispositioning could mislead downstream readers about related systems and contribution boundaries.
rationale.RX.5: Responsibility includes preserving traceability to the artifact's stated comparison axes and claims.
